_comment: Moneyfacts UK monthly headline average 2y fixed-rate residential mortgage (overall all-LTV).

methodology.indicator: Moneyfacts overall all-LTV 2y fixed average rate, beginning-of-month figure
methodology.release_cadence: monthly + occasional mid-month updates (we use the beginning-of-month figure)
methodology.extraction_notes: Series compiled from dated Mortgage Finance Gazette articles quoting Moneyfacts Rate Watch and Moneyfacts UK Mortgage Trends Treasury Report. Each point uses the start-of-month / earliest-week reading published in the relevant month. Notable moves: (1) Jul 2024 -> Aug 2024 fell 18bps (5.95% -> 5.77%) per MFG 02-08-2024 ('Two-year rates fell 18 basis points to 5.77% between the start of July and the start of August'); (2) Aug 2024 -> Sep 2024 fell 21bps (5.77% -> 5.56%) per MFG 09-09-2024; (3) Oct 2024 -> Dec 2024 climbed (5.40% -> 5.52%) on volatile swap rates; (4) Jan 2025 = 5.48%, falling steadily through 2025; (5) Aug 2025 first month under 5%; (6) Dec 2025 = 4.82-4.86% (lowest since Sept 2022 mini-Budget); (7) MAJOR EVENT: Iran conflict broke out 26 Feb 2026 — average 2y fix spiked from 4.83% (26 Feb 2026) to 5.10% mid-Mar (MFG 13-03-2026), 5.56% (25 Mar), 5.75% (27 Mar), 5.89% (2 Apr 2026). April 2026 figure of 5.89% represents a ~106bps month-on-month rise — auditable via MFG 02-04-2026. (8) Late April 2026 saw rates ease to 5.81% (MFG 24-04-2026) but the start-of-April reading is the headline figure used here. Cross-checked anchors against moneyfactscompare.co.uk, mortgagesolutions.co.uk and moneyfactsgroup.co.uk press archive. No interpolation; every point cites a dated Mortgage Finance Gazette permalink that quotes Moneyfacts directly.
